corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

top_candidates.sentence: The CIFAR-10 architecture and hyper-parameter settings on the Caffe website are fairly standard and were used here as a baseline.
There is clear performance improvement when using CLR with this architecture containing sigmoids and batch normalization.
The GoogLeNet architecture was a winning entry to the ImageNet 2014 image classification competition.
Szegedy et al. describe the architecture in detail but did not provide the architecture file.
The architecture file publicly available from Princeton was used in the following experiments.

The GoogLeNet paper does not state the learning rate values and the hyper-parameter solver file is not avail- able for a baseline but not having these hyper-parameters is a typical situation when one is developing a new architecture or applying a network to a new dataset.
Since the architecture uses a batchsize of 128 an epoch is equal to 1, 281, 167/128 = 10, 009 iterations.
Figure compares the results of running with the f ixed versus the triangular2 policy for this architecture (due to time limitations, each training stage was not run until it fully plateaued).
Training with cyclical learning rates instead of fixed values achieves improved classification accuracy without a need to tune and often in fewer iterations.
Training with cyclical learning rates instead of fixed values achieves improved classification accuracy without a need to tune and often in fewer iterations.

In addition, cyclical learning rates are demonstrated on the CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 datasets with ResNets, Stochastic Depth networks, and DenseNets, and the ImageNet dataset with the AlexNet and GoogLeNet architectures.

Deep neural networks are the basis of state-of-the-art results for image recognition , object detection , face recognition , speech recognition , machine translation , image caption generation , and driverless car technology .
It is well known that too small a learning rate will make a training algorithm converge slowly while too large a learning rate will make the training algorithm diverge .
In addition, this cyclical learning rate (CLR) method practically eliminates the need to tune the learning rate yet achieve near optimal classification accuracy.
The potential benefits of CLR can be seen in Figure , which shows the test data classification accuracy of the CIFAR-10 dataset during training 1 .
The potential benefits of CLR can be seen in Figure , which shows the test data classification accuracy of the CIFAR-10 dataset during training 1 .
The baseline (blue curve) reaches a final accuracy of 81.4% after 70, 000 iterations.
In contrast, it is possible to fully train the network using the CLR method instead of tuning (red curve) within 25,000 iterations and attain the same accuracy.

Gulcehre and Bengio propose an adaptive learning rate algorithm, called AdaSecant, that utilizes the 2 tijmen/csc321/slides/lecture slides lec6.pdf root mean square statistics and variance of the gradients.

Gulcehre and Bengio propose an adaptive learning rate algorithm, called AdaSecant, that utilizes the 2 tijmen/csc321/slides/lecture slides lec6.pdf root mean square statistics and variance of the gradients.
Kingma and Lei-Ba introduce Adam that is designed to combine the advantages from Ada-Grad and RMSProp.
Bache, et al. propose exploiting solutions to a multi-armed bandit problem for learning rate selection.

As shown in Table , one obtains the same test classification accuracy of 81.4% after only 25, 000 iterations with the triangular2 policy as obtained by running the standard hyper-parameter settings for 70, 000 iterations.
One might speculate that the benefits from the triangular policy derive from reducing the learning rate because this is when the accuracy climbs the most.
Table shows that the final accuracy is only 78.5%, providing evidence that both increasing and decreasing the learning rate are essential for the benefits of the CLR method.
